vulnerability.reference: Professional pipelines rely on automated scaling, so compound errors go unnoticed until final output.
vulnerability.generated: Production pipelines lack frame-wise temporal validation when using slice-based editing, allowing slice-level errors to compound undetected at high resolutions.

impact.reference: Release of flawed commercial videos risks brand damage and loss of viewer trust.
impact.generated: Corrupted final video outputs cause client rejection, wasted rendering resources, and reputational harm for professional studios.

dose_response.reference: Slice-level errors compounding across resolution scales in diffusion-based editing, when used in professional high-resolution pipelines, go unnoticed due to automated scaling, leading to corrupted commercial videos and brand damage.
dose_response.generated: Spatio-temporal slice editing by pretrained T2I models without frame-wise constraints produces temporal inconsistencies that, through professional studios' AI-assisted editing, trigger undetected slice-level error compounding from absent frame-wise validation, leading to corrupted outputs, client rejection, wasted resources, and reputational damage.
